spec.reasoning: We apply two safe anchor types: lexical (column renames) and distractor_column. For lexical renames: 'Year' (Sheet1 col K) and 'Year ' (Sheet2 col B) are both renamed to 'Period'/'Period ' to maintain alignment. 'Class' becomes 'Category' and 'units' becomes 'volume' — both same descriptiveness level. We do NOT rename 'State ' because the instruction mentions 'states' as a general English word. The year columns (2015-2021) are literal year numbers and are left unchanged. We also do NOT rename sheets since the instruction explicitly references 'Sheet 1' and 'Sheet 2'. A distractor column 'Volume Index' is added after the last column in Sheet1 — plausibly similar to 'volume' (the renamed 'units') but clearly different. No blank_row_insert is used to avoid the layout_impact failure from the previous attempt, respecting the multi_sheet_references risk flag.
